Author: Jamie Wright
Property: 87 Marsden Street, Parramatta, NSW 2150
Scope of Works:
Dear Customer,

We hope you're doing well. Following our routine annual testing, we have identified the following defect(s) that we wanted to bring to your attention, please see below:

Section 2 – Assessment results and observations
1.  Occupant Warning System
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
-  The occupant warning system can’t be heard from the ground floor outside the lifts. An occupant warning speaker is required in this area to prevent people entering the building during a fire.
-  The DB reading for the occupant warning level on the balcony of level 3 was too low, refer to testing records.

**We have not allowed for any upgrade to the amp due to the size of the circuit, if this is required further estimates will be provided for the rectification.**

2.  Automatic Fire Detection System
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
-  There is no zone block plan installed at the main fire panel. The matrix is required for the mechanical services and a block plan created. The site has AS1668 detection installed and Level 3: BCA 2022, AS 1640.1 - 2018, Fire
Engineering Report No. 390662-L387MarsdenSt-LoteFER-RevC, (Level 3) prepared by Lote Consulting Pty Ltd dated 08 Aug 2024
-  The AS1668 fire detection installed in the top of the fire stairs, plant rooms, and electrical cupboards on every level did not activate. The entire smoke detection system needs to be reviewed and made operational; these are critical defects that need to be addressed ASAP. Level 3 and level 4 have had new AS1670 detection installed, it appears that they have disconnected the remaining detection for the entire building during the fit-out work.
-  L2, disabled toilet, the detector was found to have a yellow cap over the detector preventing the detector from activating. The cap needs to be removed.
-  The indication light for the mechanical controls is not working and needs to be rectified (refer to testing records). The mech indicator is showing normal when the system is in fire mode. When re set an open circuit fault appears.

**We have not allowed for the installation of extra loop cards, etc in the FIP, if they are required due to the size of the circuit may require further estimates to be submitted**

10.  Paths of Travel
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
-  The two balcony doors on level 6, L2, L3 and L4, lock from the side of egress with an exit sign above the door. Access must always be available from the side of egress for all required exit doors. The doors must be connected to a fire trip to open on a fire alarm or have a single action downward handle lever lock set installed. The FER for level 2 and 3 mentions that the balcony door shall comply with BCA D3D24 and NSW D3D24. Investigation require, this may require other contractors/security.

**Kindly note further quote may be required after initial investigation.** If you have any questions or need further clarification, feel free to reach out.

Thank you for your understanding and continued support.

Author: Jade Ogony
Property: 16 Rodborough Rd, Frenchs Forest, NSW 2086
Scope of Works:
**Comprehensive Fire Alarm System Testing & Maintenance**
Ensure your building is fully compliant and safe with our expert 5-yearly sensitivity testing of the fire detection system, in line with the Fire Alarm standards AS 1851.6-2012. Our team will perform a thorough inspection of both the detection system and the Fire Indicator Panel, ensuring that everything operates smoothly and efficiently.

**Enhanced Sound System Testing for Emergencies**
Safety is our priority. We’ll test the sound pressure levels and intelligibility of your emergency sound system in all buildings to ensure your building meets the Australian standards. 

**What’s Not Included**:
Please note that the following are outside the scope of this service:
•	Security and parking arrangements (if required).

Scope of Works:
As per scope of Works

**Section 2 – Assessment results and observations**

2. **Automatic Fire Detection System & EWIS**
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliances were observed that are required to be rectified:
- There are several faults and isolation on the fire panel due to the construction works on ground level that are affecting the performance of the detection and occupant warning system (refer to service report).
- The smoke detection in the car park and main switch rooms does not activate the occupant warning system, further investigation is required.
- All BGA’s failed to activate the occupant warning system; further investigation is required.
- The red BGA at the car park entry is wired incorrectly for a red BGA, the BGA needs to be changed to white or re wired.
- The strobe failed to operate when the BGA on the fire panel was activated, further investigation is required.
- The PA failed to work in the car park, further investigation is required.

**Detection**
Location
- Switch Room, Under Construction - L1D2
- Switch Room, Check programming of FIP L1D1

**EWIS □ / OWS **
Ground Floor - Under construction
Water Re-Use Pump Room - No access
Basement 2, North Exit - BGA & WIP not EVAC on EWIS needs to be programmed in panel
Basement 2, O/S Lift - BGA & WIP not EVAC on EWIS needs to be programmed in panel
Basement 1, Ramp Out - BGA - Is meant to be white BGA and did not operate EWIS.
Level 1 - Under construction

Further estimate maybe required due to construction, and any major issues located

Property: Labour Council of NSW - 377-383 Sussex Street, Sydney, NSW 2000
Client: Unions NSW Property and Services Limited
Scope of Works: x2 attendance – x1 for deactivation & x1 for activation one floor area (tenancy & common area ) within business hours .

Author: Jamie Wright
Property: 83-87 Market Street, Sydney, NSW 2000
Scope of Works:
2. **EWIS**
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliance was observed that are required to be rectified:
- The hose reel cabinet on level 1 is missing the EWIS sign to show the location of the EWIS phone.
- The occupant warning system and PA did not work in the roof top plant room or lift motor room. Further investigation is required.  
8. **Automatic Fire Detection System**
At the time of assessment, the following non-compliance was observed that are required to be rectified:
- The basement level electrical cupboard has a cap on the detector, basement level and jewellery shop has not been tested (not done from 2023).
- L1 has a smoke detector hanging from the ceiling (not done from 2023).
- The fire detection block plan has had many changes, the block plan needs to be updated to include the amendments and change of zones. When the new block plan is created the WIP and break glass on each level needs to be identified on the block plan.
- The detectors on level 6 are all tapped up or capped off, the tape needs to be removed and re attendance is required to test the new detection on this level.
